auto_approve.mechanism: Amp does not document pre-configured auto-approval for specific MCP tools separate from tool filtering

enterprise_management.mechanism: enterprise_registry_allowlist: Amp supports organization-level MCP configuration with an enterprise registry and allowlist

env_var_expansion.mechanism: Amp MCP configuration does not document environment variable expansion

marketplace.mechanism: Amp does not have an in-IDE MCP marketplace

oauth_support.mechanism: oauth_support: Amp supports OAuth 2.0 authentication for remote MCP servers

resource_referencing.mechanism: Amp does not document MCP resource referencing

tool_filtering.mechanism: per_tool_enable_disable: Amp supports per-tool enable/disable configuration to control which MCP tools are exposed to the agent

transport_types.mechanism: Amp MCP transport types not documented beyond basic MCP support

providerExtensions.name: MCP server configuration: amp.mcpServers
providerExtensions.summary: Servers configured under 'amp.mcpServers' with local (command/args/env) or remote (url/headers) modes; supports '$' env-var refs and 'disabled' flag.

providerExtensions.name: Settings file locations by platform
providerExtensions.summary: Settings live at '%APPDATA%\amp\settings.json' (Windows) or '~/.config/amp/settings.json' (mac/Linux); workspace at '.amp/settings.json'.

providerExtensions.name: CLI: amp mcp add
providerExtensions.summary: Add servers via 'amp mcp add <name> -- <command> [args...]' (local) or 'amp mcp add <name> <url>' (remote); writes to user settings.

providerExtensions.name: MCP server loading precedence
providerExtensions.summary: On name collisions, precedence is CLI flags > user/workspace config > skill-bundled servers.

providerExtensions.name: Workspace MCP server trust approval
providerExtensions.summary: Workspace-defined servers require 'amp mcp approve <name>' before running; global and CLI-provided servers run without approval.

providerExtensions.name: OAuth authentication for remote MCP servers
providerExtensions.summary: OAuth flow auto-starts for self-registering servers; manual setup uses 'amp mcp oauth login'; tokens cached in '~/.amp/oauth/'.

providerExtensions.name: Per-tool enable/disable
providerExtensions.summary: Individual tools within a configured MCP server can be disabled (via VS Code UI) without removing the server.

providerExtensions.name: VS Code extension UI for MCP setup
providerExtensions.summary: VS Code Amp extension exposes MCP setup via Settings > Extensions > Amp > MCP Servers > Add MCP Server.

providerExtensions.name: Skill-bundled MCP servers as preferred approach
providerExtensions.summary: Official guidance prefers bundling MCP servers in skills via mcp.json over global config to keep tool-lists clean.

providerExtensions.name: Enterprise MCP registry allowlist
providerExtensions.summary: Workspace admins enforce an approved-MCP registry (modelcontextprotocol.io schema); unreachable registry blocks all servers.
